title: Multi-file feature: rate limiter middleware + store + tests
strength: Parallel decomposition across multiple new files with a shared contract.
prompt: In this empty Node project, implement a token-bucket rate limiter as Express middleware. Create three files: src/store.js (an in-memory token-bucket store with `take(key, cost)` returning {allowed, remaining, resetMs}, configurable capacity and refillPerSec), src/middleware.js (Express middleware factory `rateLimit({capacity, refillPerSec, keyFn})` that uses the store, sets X-RateLimit-Remaining header, and returns HTTP 429 with a JSON body {error:'rate_limited'} when exhausted), and src/index.js (re-exports both). Use CommonJS modules. Write Jest tests in test/store.test.js and test/middleware.test.js covering: bucket starts full, take() decrements, exhaustion returns allowed=false, refill over time restores tokens, and the middleware sets headers + returns 429. Wire up package.json scripts `test` (jest) and `lint` (eslint src test). Add a minimal eslint flat config. Everything must pass.
setup: npm install --no-audit --no-fund jest eslint express supertest >/dev/null 2>&1
